note: Compare to the peer Critic, which said PROCEED on this same Orienter step.

orienter.anticipates_interaction: names the lr×bs interaction in uncertainties but does NOT act on it — the approach is two sequential single-axis sweeps.
orienter.error_note: The approach seeds the freeze: it fixes bs=512 then sweeps lr, then fixes lr LOW (4.883e-4) and sweeps bs, so the second sweep can never reach the high-lr × large-bs corner.
orienter.decision_error_or_information_gap: decision-error — it had already articulated the interaction a priori, so fixing one axis low was a knowable design flaw, not bad luck.
